title: The Prismatic Lodge

features.name: the-first-room-and-its-twin
features.desc-on-touch: a room splits into its mirror image mid-space; both versions are real; you occupy both simultaneously

features.name: the-corners-that-multiply
features.desc-on-touch: each angle refracts into more angles; the architecture becomes a fractal; you count the corners and never reach the same number twice

features.name: the-light-that-splits-by-angle
features.desc-on-touch: a single source of light refracts through each room differently; in one corner it arrives as day, in the adjacent refraction as dusk, as storm, as underlit storage

features.name: the-doorways-that-choose-their-destinations
features.desc-on-touch: each exit leads to a version of the lodge where different walls are bearing weight; a door north in one refraction leads south in its twin

features.name: the-eucalyptus-growing-through-rooms
features.desc-on-touch: the render is remembering the ground—a burled trunk grows through multiple refractions at once, its bark holding the angles together

inscription: each angle holds a different house; the render cannot collapse them, so every refracted twin sleeps in the walls and wakes in the mirrors

file.name: Katita_Refraction_Study_Notes.txt
file.note: observations on prismatic space; marginal annotation in biro: 'does the house know it is splitting, or does it think the world finally has enough room'

exits.label: back down the fracture into the four-angled space
into the refracted distance where the lodge continues fragmenting forever
into the twin-lodge where all the rejected angles live
